headline: Transcript: Claer Barrett on spending wisely during the holidays
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Claer Barrett
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Claer Barrett on spending wisely during the holidays’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Friday, December 19th, and this is your FT News Briefing.

Yesterday’s US inflation report is being questioned by economists. Plus, we asked you how your spending has changed this holiday season. You answered. Our consumer editor, Claer Barrett, gives her thoughts.

I’m Marc Filippino, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Inflation in the US unexpectedly dipped to 2.7 per cent in November. The report that came out yesterday showed a number well below the 3.1 per cent figure economist predicted. And economists, well, they think something’s up with a report. Namely, that the government shut down that ran from October 1st until November 12th skewed the numbers.
